name: CRUD/update
description: Instance update (PUT) conformance tests: version bump on content change vs. no bump on no-op updates, malformed Content-Type/If-Match rejection, client-supplied-id upsert-create, URL/body id-mismatch and missing-id rejection, and conditional-reference resolution via identifier (from UpdateTests).

variable.name: createdVersionId
variable.description: versionId assigned to fhir262-update-content when it was first PUT in setup

variable.name: versionAfterContentChange
variable.description: versionId assigned after the content-changing update in the first test; used to prove no-op updates keep this same version and as the correct If-Match precondition later

variable.name: mismatchPatientId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient created in setup for the URL/body id mismatch test

variable.name: invalidETag
variable.description: A malformed If-Match value. Parametrized by the http://ignixa.io/testscript/parametrize engine extension — engines that do not support the extension execute the test once using this defaultValue.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content (Version A) to establish the resource for the content-change/no-op/etag tests
POST a Patient with a server-assigned id, used later to demonstrate a URL/body id mismatch
PUT Practitioner/fhir262-update-prac1, referenced later via a conditional (identifier) reference

test.name: update with changed content assigns a new version and preserves the id
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with different content (Version B) must return 200 OK, keep the same id, and assign a new meta.versionId different from the one assigned at setup.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with Version B content

assert.description: Response body must be a Patient
meta.versionId must differ from the version assigned at setup

test.name: repeating an identical update does not create a new version
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content again with the exact same content (Version B) must return 200 OK and must NOT bump meta.versionId, since there is no actual data change.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content again with the identical Version B content

assert.description: meta.versionId must be unchanged (no data change means no new version)

test.name: update with a malformed Content-Type header is rejected
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with an unparseable Content-Type ('Jibberish') must return 400 Bad Request rather than attempting to parse the body.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with Content-Type: Jibberish

test.name: update with a client-supplied id for a non-existent resource performs an upsert-create
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-newpat, which does not yet exist, must return 201 Created, assign the client-supplied id, and override the client-supplied meta.versionId ('9999') and lastUpdated with server-assigned values (FHIR R4 upsert-create semantics).

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-newpat with client-supplied meta.versionId=9999

assert.description: Response body must be a Patient
id must match the client-supplied id
Server must have assigned a meta.versionId
Server must override the client-supplied meta.versionId of '9999'

test.name: update with a body containing no id returns 400
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-noid-target with a body that has no 'id' element at all must return 400 Bad Request (FHIR R4 requires the resource id to match the URL id).

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-noid-target with a body lacking 'id'

test.name: update to a URL id that disagrees with the body id returns 400
test.description: Read back the Patient created in setup, then PUT that body (which has its own id) to a different URL id. The server must reject this with 400 Bad Request because the logical id in the URL and the body disagree (FHIR R4 §2.1.0.6).

operation.description: Read back the Patient created in setup to use its body (with its own id) as the update payload
PUT the read-back body (id=${mismatchPatientId}) to a deliberately different URL id

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request when URL id and body id disagree

test.name: update with a correct If-Match version precondition succeeds
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with an If-Match header matching the resource's current meta.versionId must succeed with 200 OK.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with If-Match matching the current versionId

test.name: update with a malformed If-Match header value returns 400
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with an If-Match value that is not a well-formed weak ETag (a bare quoted string, a negative number, or zero) must return 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with a malformed If-Match value

test.name: update with a well-formed but stale If-Match version returns 412 Precondition Failed
test.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with an If-Match version number that is well-formed but never assigned to the resource must return 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-content with If-Match: W/"999999" (never the current version)

test.name: conditional reference by identifier on generalPractitioner resolves correctly
test.description: PUT a Patient whose generalPractitioner reference is a conditional (identifier-based) search reference to the Practitioner created in setup. The server must resolve it to the concrete Practitioner/fhir262-update-prac1 reference.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-update-patgp1 with a conditional generalPractitioner reference

assert.description: Response body must be a Patient
generalPractitioner reference must resolve to the concrete Practitioner reference

operation.description: Teardown: delete the content-change/etag test Patient
Teardown: delete the upsert-create Patient
Teardown: delete the mismatch-test Patient
Teardown: delete the generalPractitioner-reference Patient
Teardown: delete the referenced Practitioner
Teardown: defensively attempt to delete the no-id-body target in case a lenient server created it (expected to already be absent)
